office_does: The clerk preserves Superior Court records and manages court-related deposits, disbursements, filing access, and administrative systems.
race_blurb: Four-term incumbent Scott Weber runs on continuity and court technology; attorney Rachel Shapiro emphasizes accessibility and transparency, while attorney Gerald Gray emphasizes courtroom experience.

candidates.summary: The four-term incumbent clerk, offering a specific record on court technology, access, restitution collection, and service delivery.
highlights: Says an independent imaging system kept the court operating during the 2024 statewide outage [S1]
Reports improvements to restitution collection, real-time document access, and electronic filing [S1]
Emphasizes record integrity, open access, efficiency, and responsive service [S1]

spending.basis: Centers efficiency and operational systems rather than program expansion.

experience.basis: Has led the clerk's office since 2010 and cites a concrete 16-year administrative and technology record.

candidates.summary: An attorney and mediator proposing easier access to records and financial information plus regular public office hours.
highlights: Promises accurate records, timely service, accessible information, and a more user-friendly office [S1]
Supports easier online budget and spending information and monthly public office hours [S1]

spending.basis: Her clearest fiscal plank is easier public access to budget and spending information.

experience.basis: Brings legal and mediation experience but no documented clerk-office administration or elected record.

candidates.summary: A longtime attorney with prosecution, defense, military-law, and voting-officer experience who emphasizes accurate records and efficient administration.
highlights: Reports more than 25 years as an attorney across prosecution, defense, and Navy JAG work [S1]
Emphasizes accurate court records, efficient administration, and retaining an elected clerk [S1]

experience.basis: Has extensive court-facing legal experience but no documented clerk-office administration, a mixed record-versus-renewal profile.
